title: ingest: capture real video title (mp4 tags empty) + backfill + title-based output folder
description: ingest.sh reads title from mp4 container tags which yt-dlp doesn't set, so ingest.json title='' -> source-credit shows the source-id and all shorts pile into output/source/. Fix: embed-metadata + print-to-file title at download; backfill existing work/*/ingest.json from mcptube.db; make save-local/start.sh use the real title for the output folder.

title: start.sh bricks CPU: unbounded span fan-out + uncapped -threads 8 per process on 8-core machine
description: Root cause of pipeline CPU brick. (1) max_par (SHORTS_MAX_PAR:-8) at start.sh:33 is declared but never used; phase 2/3/4 fan-out (start.sh:561-599) launches ALL count spans concurrently with no throttle. (2) Every ffmpeg uses -threads 8 (encode.sh:60) and whisper-cli (transcribe.sh:47) has no thread cap -> all 8 cores each. ~5 spans x 8 threads = ~40 threads on 8 cores -> thrash/100% sustained. Fix: enforce max_par in fan-out loops (default ~2), and scale SHORTS_THREADS ~= cores/max_par + add whisper --threads. Machine: hw.ncpu=8.
notes: FIXED. start.sh:33 default SHORTS_MAX_PAR now 1 (sequential). Added adaptive thread budget: SHORTS_THREADS/SHORTS_WHISPER_THREADS = (logicalcpu-1)/max_par (=7 for sequential on this 8-core mac). Added bash-3.2 throttle() (no wait -n) gating all 3 fan-out loops (phase 2/3/4). transcribe.sh now passes --threads. encode.sh vt_threads fallback 8->4. Verified: bash -n on all 3 files, throttle smoke test (4x1s jobs @max_par=2 = 2s), empty-array safe under set -u, whisper-cli --threads confirmed supported. REMAINING: verify on a real pipeline run.

title: Fix /start handling of multiple video IDs
description: /start currently forwards all slash-command arguments as one quoted string to start.sh. Running /start with several YouTube IDs makes yt-dlp receive one invalid URL. Fix the command/script boundary so multiple inputs are handled intentionally instead of failing in srcprep.
close_reason: Handled batch /start argument splitting and bare YouTube ID normalization.

title: Use persistent Claude panes in start orchestrator
description: Update start.sh and Claude-driven skills so related semantic steps run through long-lived tmux Claude lanes, preserving context within a source/span while clearing at lane boundaries. Route verify-bookends and pick-mood through the pane helper too.
close_reason: Implemented persistent chat-mode tmux panes for start orchestration, routed verify-bookends and pick-mood through pane helper, and documented lane-clear behavior.

title: pane sentinel race in --pane mode: orchestrator reads out.txt while claude still running
description:
The /start orchestrator's pane driver (.claude/skills/_lib/pane.sh run_claude_step) has a race where out.done can appear (briefly) before claude -p has actually produced its output, so the orchestrator copies an empty out.txt to the reply file and parse_reply.py fails.

REPRO (deterministic on a 4-span run against an already-ingested source):
  rm -rf work/883ad50ade/_pane work/883ad50ade/clip_* work/883ad50ade/segments.{coherent,}.json
  SHORTS_N=2 SHORTS_DMIN=15 SHORTS_DMAX=40 ./start.sh 883ad50ade

OBSERVED: 4 editor panes run in parallel. Span 1 (first to enter trim-filler) succeeds. Spans 2/3/4 all log
  parse_reply: no JSON object in reply:
  [hh:mm:ss] [phase 2 / span NN] trim-filler FAILED

FORENSICS — stat -f %SB on the failing spans' trim-filler sentinels:
  span 02 out.txt birth=12:03:50 mod=12:04:01  out.done birth=12:03:50 mod=12:04:02
  span 03 out.txt birth=12:04:39 mod=12:04:48  out.done birth=12:04:39 mod=12:04:48
  span 04 out.txt birth=12:04:35 mod=12:04:42  out.done birth=12:04:35 mod=12:04:43

  vs successful span 01:
  span 01 out.txt birth=12:02:26 mod=12:02:38  out.done birth=12:02:38 mod=12:02:38

For the failing spans, out.txt AND out.done are both born at the same instant (when the shell sets up the > redirect), then 7-11 seconds later claude actually writes 201-bytes of valid JSON and re-touches out.done. The orchestrator catches the FIRST appearance of out.done (the empty-file-just-created state) and copies an empty out.txt.

The exact mechanism by which the redirect's birth time equals the touch time on the failing runs (vs being correctly ordered on span 01) is unclear — possibly tmux send-keys + bash on a busy pane causes the cmd to be perceived as executing twice, or the cmd is processed in two pieces. Single-pane / serial runs (shorts.sh non-interactive path) do NOT hit this, only parallel --pane mode does. Claude's own log file is empty for the failing runs — claude either ran twice with the first being a silent no-op, or the redirect was set up at one time and claude actually wrote at another.

OUTCOME of end-to-end run: 1/4 shorts saved (output/source/short_01.mp4 from span 01); spans 2-4 dropped at phase 2/3 boundary per spec.

FIX OPTIONS to evaluate:
1. Make the sentinel carry a unique token written by the pane cmd, orchestrator waits until out.done contains the expected token (rules out empty/stale done).
2. Wait for out.done AND require out.txt to be non-empty before reading (won't help if Claude's actual response is legitimately empty, but at least catches the race).
3. Validate parsed reply in the skill; on empty reply, sleep 1s and re-read once before declaring failure.
4. Investigate the underlying tmux+bash send-keys behavior that produces the simultaneous birth times for failing spans.

REGRESSION SCOPE: only affects --pane mode (introduced in shorts-hsu). Headless claude -p path (shorts.sh) is unaffected.
notes:
=== Investigation 2026-05-26 (after user theory check) ===

User theory: previous-round out.done not cleaned before next round.
VERIFIED FALSE: pane.sh:54 already does `rm -f "$dir/out.done" "$dir/out.txt"` before cp prompt + send-keys. And $dir is per-step (.../editor-NN/<step>/) so cross-step contamination within a pane isn't possible either.

=== Reproduction attempts in isolation — ALL CLEAN ===

1. 4 parallel panes, simple fast cmd (sleep+cat): clean. out.txt born before out.done in every pane.
2. Single pane, two rounds with 55s delay between: clean.
3. 4 parallel panes, real `claude -p` calls in parallel: clean.
4. 4 parallel panes, two-round claude calls with 45s delay between rounds: clean.
5. Single pane, ACTUAL trim-filler prompt from the failed span 02 (2769 bytes), preceded by a bookend-trim-like first round + 55s delay: clean.

In every isolated test, out.txt birth time < out.done birth time, content correct.

=== What I cannot explain ===

In the actual e2e orchestrator run (start.sh on 883ad50ade), for spans 2/3/4 the forensics show:
- out.txt and out.done both BORN at the same instant (within 1s)
- out.txt MODIFIED 7-11s later with the real claude reply
- out.done MODIFIED 1s after the out.txt modification
This pattern is consistent with the cmd executing TWICE: once instant-fast (creating both files empty/empty), once normally (writing real content, re-touching done). But I cannot identify what caused the cmd to execute twice and cannot reproduce the double-execution.

The runtime conditions that differ between my isolated tests and the real run that I have NOT yet ruled out:
- system load: actual run had ffmpeg cut-clip + Python rebase running concurrently in the orchestrator subshells during the gap between bookend-trim and trim-filler
- tmux send-keys interaction with bash readline when the pane is being driven by a different subprocess than my isolated tests
- prompts coming through claude -p with mcptube MCP server registered globally (every claude -p loads MCP servers at startup; if mcptube was slow to handshake, claude might appear to do something then "redo" it)

=== Fix options reconsidered ===

The cleanest race-immune fix would be a unique-token sentinel: orchestrator generates UUID, pane cmd writes that UUID to out.done (not just touch), orchestrator waits for out.done to contain the expected UUID. Stale or empty out.done is ignored. This is robust regardless of root cause — IF the cmd were to "run twice", only the second run's token would match the expected one.

But: I cannot reproduce the failure, so I cannot verify the fix actually addresses what was happening. Per user instruction ("Do NOT push speculative fixes — only push if there's a clear regression introduced by your earlier changes; if the root cause is something else and the fix isn't obvious, leave shorts-tnd open"), holding off.

=== Status ===

Open. Waiting on either:
(a) a reproducer that exhibits the double-cmd-execution behavior, OR
(b) a decision to ship the unique-token sentinel as a defensive hardening pass even without confirmed root cause.

E2E result on this run: 1/4 shorts saved (output/source/short_01.mp4). Spec ships partially — orchestration scaffolding works, but parallel --pane mode is unreliable on the trim-filler step (and likely other Claude-driven steps after the first per-pane invocation).

title: Section 1: /start orchestrator with parallel tmux panes
description:
New top-level entry point /start <url|source-id> that fans the pipeline across long-lived tmux panes. Per May26-spec.md section 1.

Components:
1. /start slash command at .claude/commands/start.md (or equivalent) — invokes an orchestrator script
2. Orchestrator script (e.g. start.sh) that:
   - Preflights mcptube MCP server at http://127.0.0.1:9093/mcp (HTTP probe, 406 on GET = healthy). Abort with instructions if unreachable.
   - Spawns deterministic panes: shorts-<source-id>-<phase>-<i>
   - Phase 1 parallel: pane src-prep (bash: ingest+transcribe); pane crew-analysis (Claude with mcptube MCP available: mcptube add <url>, then waits for transcript.json, then segment-topics + pick-segments + verify-coherence)
   - Phase 2 per-span parallel: pane crew-editor-i runs bookend-trim -> cut-clip+rebase -> clear-and-talk -> trim-filler -> cut-filler+tighten-pace -> clear-and-talk -> verify-bookends -> fit-vertical
   - Phase 3 per-span parallel pair: crew-captions-i (chunk-captions -> burn-subtitles -> clear-and-talk -> generate-title -> title-transition -> loudnorm) AND crew-broll-i (broll-pick only)
   - Phase 4 per-span: completion-i (broll-composite -> like-subscribe-overlay -> bg-music -> qc-clip -> save-local)
3. Pane driver protocol: orchestrator writes work/<id>/_pane/<pane>/in.txt, sends 'cat <path> | claude' (or equivalent) via tmux send-keys, waits on out.done sentinel, reads out.txt. Sentinel protocol: write out.txt first, sync, touch out.done.
4. Add --pane <name> mode to 8 Claude-driven skill .sh wrappers: segment-topics, pick-segments, verify-coherence, bookend-trim, trim-filler, chunk-captions, generate-title, broll-pick. Without --pane, headless claude -p remains the default behavior.
5. Failure handling: span failed in phase 2 = skip phase 3/4 panes; failed in phase 3 = skip phase 4. Surface in summary. Orchestrator exits cleanly on fatal failure: kills outstanding panes, prints failed span, leaves JSON on disk.
6. /start <source-id> mode: skip ingest if source.mp4+transcript.json exist.
7. Re-run overwrites existing output/<source>/ shorts.
8. Foreground prints '[phase N / span M] skill running...' style updates.

Depends on section 2's broll-pick/broll-composite split (file beads as blocker).
Depends on section 3's verify-bookends skill (file beads as blocker).

Full spec at /Users/jperr/Documents/shorts/May26-spec.md section 1.

title: Section 3: within-short editing pass
description:
Add verify-bookends skill + tighten trim-filler aggressiveness + lower tighten-pace defaults. Per May26-spec.md section 3.

Three axes:
A. NEW skill .claude/skills/verify-bookends/ — runs after tighten-pace, before fit-vertical. Claude sees first/last ~1.5s of trimmed transcript + 3-frame strip from each end. Returns {action:keep} or {action:trim, t0, t1}. INWARD-ONLY adjustments. If proposed trim makes short <15s, reject. Apply via second cut-clip + rebase. Drop span if can't clean without removing >2s.
B. Tighten trim-filler — update build_prompt.py to: treat standard filler list (um/uh/like/you know/sort of/kind of/I mean/basically) as always-removable not judgment; collapse repeated re-starts (2+) to last take only; aggressive removal of digressive asides; hard ceiling — never increase span duration (fallback to no-op if Claude's keeps exceed input duration); warn if >40% removed.
C. tighten-pace defaults — change defaults: gap=0.18 (from 0.25), add sentence_beat=0.15, collapse_to=0.08. Sentence boundaries detected via . ? ! on the word before a gap. Preserve env overrides TIGHTEN_GAP/TIGHTEN_SENTENCE_BEAT.

Order in shorts.sh editor chain: bookend-trim -> cut-clip -> rebase -> trim-filler -> cut-filler -> tighten-pace -> verify-bookends (may issue inward cut-clip+rebase) -> fit-vertical.

Full spec at /Users/jperr/Documents/shorts/May26-spec.md section 3.

title: Section 2: broll-pick/broll-composite split + vision verify
description:
Split .claude/skills/broll/ into broll-pick (Claude-driven candidate selection with vision verification) and broll-composite (pure ffmpeg overlay). Per May26-spec.md section 2.

Core changes:
- broll-pick emits broll_plan.json with {t0,t1,query,clip_path,anchor_word} entries
- broll-composite reads broll_plan.json + clip, does ffmpeg overlay only
- fetch_pexels.py returns TOP 3 candidates per query (not just #1)
- Batch vision check: one Claude call per query with 3-frame strip (start/mid/end) per candidate; Claude returns best index or 'none pass'
- Query-rewrite fallback: text-only Claude call, then second Pexels pull + second batch check
- Drop slot on double miss
- BROLL_VISION_CAP env (default 10), tracked across clip
- Duration snaps to chunk-captions boundaries (read clip_NN.chunks.json); max 5s, min 2s
- Idempotency keys split between pick (input+transcript+chunks mtimes) and composite (input+broll_plan mtime)
- broll_plan.json invalidation includes chunks mtime
- shorts.sh updated to call broll-pick then broll-composite in sequence

Out of scope: anchor detection changes in build_prompt.py, non-Pexels sources, overlay region changes.

Full spec at /Users/jperr/Documents/shorts/May26-spec.md section 2.

title: remove title-transition from canonical shorts.sh pipeline
description: Per research, the slide-in/hold/slide-out title card + stock whoosh delays the hook by 2.5s (~8% retention on a 30s short, per Jenny's 3%/s rule). It is the most generic After Effects beginner template. Remove from shorts.sh canonical chain. Replace with the new hook-overlay skill (separate bead). Keep title-transition.sh skill on disk as opt-in for non-pipeline use, but update CLAUDE.md 'Hard rules' section to reference hook-overlay instead. Must be done AFTER hook-overlay lands.
notes: Superseded by SPEC.md (commit df6ece3): title-transition kept and restyled to match captions; captions use Claude-chunked phrases (chunk-captions skill) instead of a rolling 4-word window.

title: burn-subtitles: redesign for brand recognition
description: Current style (Arial Bold + #FFD633 yellow + lower-third + ease-out-back word-karaoke) is the default of every auto-caption tool (CapCut, Submagic, Opus Clip). Per Isaac's branded-caption research, distinctiveness IS the engagement mechanism. CONSTRAINTS — do NOT pick any of: Arial, Inter, Helvetica, Roboto, SF Pro; MrBeast yellow (#FFD633 or near); plain lower-third position; ease-out-back pop animation; pure white-on-black. PICK: (a) a distinctive system or shipped font (e.g. Impact, Bebas Neue if available; or a chunky condensed sans; or an italicized display face) — verify the font file exists locally before committing, (b) a non-yellow distinctive accent (e.g. cyan, hot pink, electric green, deep red), (c) a non-lower-third position OR a position-shifting rule (e.g. mid-frame for declarations, near-mouth for reactions), (d) a non-pop animation (e.g. hard cut, slide-up-tiny, scale-from-0.9). Keep word-karaoke timing logic. Render a test frame and visually confirm it does not look like a CapCut default. Document the chosen preset in the SKILL.md so it can be the deliberate brand.

title: cut-clip: precise default + dead-tail trim
description: Per Jenny Hoyos: one wasted second = 3% retention loss; one bad final frame moved a short 50K→1M. (1) Flip cut-clip default to reencode=true (frame-accurate) — current ±1s keyframe slop is incompatible with retention-tuned editing. Stream-copy stays available as an explicit opt-in. (2) Add automatic dead-tail scan: detect trailing silence (>-40dB) and trailing near-static frames in the last 2s of the cut; trim to the last real audio/visual event. Emit a sidecar JSON noting how much was trimmed.

title: pick-segments: score opening hook strength
description: HIGHEST LEVERAGE PER RESEARCH. Rewrite pick-segments Claude prompt to grade candidates on: (a) first-1-2s scroll-stop strength (does the opening word/phrase plant a curiosity question or land a striking visual claim?), (b) hook→foreshadow→payoff structure, (c) but/therefore causality between beats, (d) open curiosity loops that resolve at the end. Reject spans whose opening word is filler ('so', 'and', 'um', 'like', 'I think', 'basically'). Emit per-span scores in segments.json so downstream selection can rank. Keep topic-coherence gate intact. Constraint: still one Claude call per request — extend the prompt, do not add a second pass.

title: Implement shorts entrypoint (URL -> finished clips)
description: Top-level driver: takes a YouTube URL, runs the full atomic-skill chain (ingest -> transcribe -> detect-faces -> pick-speaker -> pick-segments -> per-span: cut-clip -> reframe-vertical -> burn-subtitles -> loudnorm -> qc-clip -> save-local). Each skill is invoked independently; data passes via JSON on disk under ./work/<id>/. Out of scope: GUI, upload to platforms.
close_reason: Implemented and tested against work/ artifacts

title: Implement yt-dlp ingest skill
description: Download source video locally from a YouTube/URL input. Writes to ./work/<id>/source.mp4 and emits ingest.json with metadata (title, duration, fps, url). First step of pipeline.
close_reason: implemented .claude/skills/ingest/ingest.sh + SKILL.md; verified on Big Buck Bunny URL; produced work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (709M) and ingest.json {duration:634.624, fps:60, 3840x2160}

title: Implement pick-segments skill (Claude ranker)
description: Compute per-second RMS energy from source audio. Feed transcript + RMS summary to Claude, get back N spans with t0/t1/rationale/title. See .claude/skills/pick-segments/SKILL.md.
notes: Implemented .claude/skills/pick-segments/{pick-segments.sh,rms.py,build_prompt.py,parse_reply.py}. Calls claude -p (no API key, uses host session). Tested E2E on synthetic 110s transcript with 10 segments: produced 2 non-overlapping spans (0-38.5s 'The Light That Shot Straight Up', 47-95s 'It's Been Visiting For Years') with rationales — Claude correctly avoided mid-thought cuts and respected [20,60]s duration. RMS helper tested on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (10min BBB): 635 buckets, min=6e-05 max=0.093 mean=0.016. Idempotent via mtime cache (cache hit confirmed on second run). claude -p smoke test PONG verified.

title: Implement transcribe skill (whisper.cpp local)
description: Wrap whisper-cli with local GGML model into a Python script. Input video/audio path -> output JSON with words[] and segments[]. Read WHISPER_BIN and WHISPER_MODEL from .env. Extract 16kHz mono via ffmpeg, pipe to whisper-cli with --output-json-full, parse + flatten. See .claude/skills/transcribe/SKILL.md for I/O contract.
close_reason: implemented .claude/skills/transcribe/transcribe.sh; tested on 8.5s say-synthesized speech -> 23 words + 3 segments with word-level timestamps

title: Pivot stack: whisper.cpp + Claude (subscription/crew) + ffmpeg + atomic skills, local output
description: User pivot. Scrap monolithic Python pipeline; modularize every shorts-production op into atomic Claude Code skills under .claude/skills/. Stack: whisper.cpp local (with user's ggml-large-v3-turbo-q5_0.bin model) for transcription, Claude via host Code session + /crew tmux members for semantic ops (no API key — uses subscription), ffmpeg for media, MediaPipe for face boxes, optional Rails+Sidekiq wrapper. Output is local: ./output/<source-name>/. Pre-pivot code preserved on archive/pre-pivot branch.
close_reason: Pivot complete — all 11 atomic skills implemented, shorts.sh entrypoint drives the full chain

title: broll suite: broll-pick + broll-composite + broll-cleanup (mcptube)
description: Per SPEC-broll.md. Three skills under .claude/skills/{broll-pick,broll-composite,broll-cleanup}/. broll-pick: Claude anchor-pick (3-5 nouns) + interleaved chunk-snapped windows; mcptube discover->add->frame-sample->claude -p vision verify (reject/rewrite once); yt-dlp segment download to work/<id>/broll/; vision cap default 10; emits broll_plan.json. broll-composite: pure-ffmpeg full-frame hard cut, full-bleed cover crop, podcast audio stream-copied. broll-cleanup: end-of-run evict mcptube + local cache for ingested video_ids only; plan.json persists. NOTE: do NOT edit shorts.sh/start.sh/CLAUDE.md — report required chain placement (incl chunk-captions move before broll-pick) to parent for integration.

title: fill-vertical: punch-in 9:16 reframe, retire fit-vertical + reframe-vertical
description: Per SPEC-fill-vertical.md. New skill .claude/skills/fill-vertical/ (fill_vertical.py + fill-vertical.sh). Fill-frame 9:16, never letterbox. Scene-cut detect -> static crop per shot. MediaPipe FaceLandmarker lip-activity speaker pick; face ~45% height, eyeline upper third; OpenCV saliency for no-face; upscale cap 2x. Per-shot cut/crop/scale + concat, audio stream-copied. Deps: mediapipe, opencv-contrib-python, numpy. Delete fit-vertical + reframe-vertical. NOTE: do NOT edit shorts.sh/start.sh/CLAUDE.md — report required chain placement to parent for integration.

title: pick-segments: engagement tuning (prompt-only)
description: Per SPEC-pick-segments.md. Prompt-only edit to build_prompt.py SCROLL-STOP HOOK block (lines ~82-93): name engagement cues (vocal energy/affect, lively back-and-forth, concrete specificity) folded into holistic overall_score; tie energy to existing 60-bin RMS sparkline (favor peaks). NO new fields, NO new deps, NO parse_reply/rms changes, NO multi-speaker weighting. Keep added guidance tight (<~150 instr).

title: start.sh pane driver: chunk-captions hangs on Claude 'thinking block' 400 API error
description: During a /start re-run, span-02 chunk-captions in the captions Claude pane errored: 'API Error: 400 messages...thinking or redacted_thinking blocks in the latest assistant message cannot be modified.' The pane also wandered running exploratory ls/find/du before erroring, then sat idle at the prompt. phase 3 waits for all spans, so the whole video blocked. Pane driver should detect the API-error/idle-prompt state and fail (or /clear + retry) the step instead of polling out.txt until timeout. Unrelated to the title/music/folder/leak fixes in ea0b75f.

title: bg-music: add fade-in and bump default bed volume
description: Music bed exposed during silent title intro; add afade-in and raise default volume 0.12 to 0.17.

title: bg-music: add fade-in and bump default bed volume
description: Music bed starts abruptly at t=0 and is exposed during the speech-free title intro (reads as a 'noise' when the title card appears). Add a short afade-in and raise default volume 0.12 -> ~0.17 (user: slightly too quiet).

title: start.sh: per-span Claude panes leak (cleanup misses subshell-created panes)
description: pane_new appends to PANES, but per-span panes are created inside background subshells so the parent cleanup trap never sees them; only srcprep+analysis get killed. ~6 idle claude panes leak per video. Fix: cleanup kills all tmux sessions matching shorts-<id>-*.

title: source-credit skill: bottom-third Original video overlay
description: New atomic skill .claude/skills/source-credit. Renders Original video: (blue) + source title (white) as transparent PNG via PIL, overlays via ffmpeg in bottom third (y~70% of 1920) above the like-subscribe CTA. Reads title from work/<id>/ingest.json. Wires into start.sh phase-3 captions pane between title-transition and loudnorm.

title: source-credit skill: bottom-third 'Original video: <title>' overlay
description: New atomic skill .claude/skills/source-credit. Renders 'Original video:' (blue) + source title (white) as a transparent PNG via PIL, overlays via ffmpeg in the bottom third (y~70% of 1920) so it never overlaps the like-subscribe CTA banner (lower ~12%). Reads title from work/<id>/ingest.json. Wires into start.sh phase-3 captions pane between title-transition and loudnorm.

title: source-credit skill: bottom-third 'Original video: <title>' overlay
description: New atomic skill .claude/skills/source-credit. Renders 'Original video:' (blue) + source title (white) as a transparent PNG via PIL, overlays via ffmpeg in the bottom third (y~70% of 1920) so it never overlaps the like-subscribe CTA banner (lower ~12%). Reads title from work/<id>/ingest.json. Wires into start.sh phase-3 captions pane between title-transition and loudnorm.

title: Audit and remove unused b-roll pipeline skills
description: Audit b-roll skill usage before removing b-roll from the shorts pipeline so dead skills do not take space or get invoked by agents.
close_reason: Removed b-roll skills and active pipeline references after audit

title: Remove title transition sound effect
description: Title cards should render visually without adding or documenting synthesized title-card SFX.
close_reason: Removed title-transition SFX wiring and documentation; smoke-tested silent and audio inputs.

title: verify-bookends: cut-on-payoff trailing + opening-hook VVSA gate
description: Extend .claude/skills/verify-bookends to (a) trim trailing 1.5s on payoff-word + 80ms tail when a clear payoff is present, and (b) grade opening hook — would a stranger keep watching past 3s — with keep/trim-inward/drop action. Same I/O contract, one Claude call.

title: generate-title: rewrite prompt to hook-driven (3-framing, payoff-tease, 5th-grade)
description: Rewrite .claude/skills/generate-title/build_prompt.py per spec + scarnon corrections: detect-then-tease, three framings (concrete-claim/specific-question/contradiction), subject-naming, 5th-grade vocab, payoff-word ban, silent-comprehension test, ban vague pronouns + clickbait intensifiers.

title: VideoToolbox HW accel + thread cap sweep across ffmpeg skills
description: Apply scarnon research findings: switch libx264 software encodes to h264_videotoolbox, add -hwaccel videotoolbox on decode, cap threads, bump intermediate CRFs/bitrates. Affects cut-clip, cut-filler, tighten-pace, fit-vertical, burn-subtitles, title-transition, broll-composite, broll.

title: trim-filler + cut-filler skills
description: Two atomic skills for the per-clip editing chain. trim-filler: Claude reads a clip transcript and marks filler/trail-offs/false-starts; emits keeps.json + transcript.trimmed.json. cut-filler: re-encodes the clip via ffmpeg select/aselect using keeps.json. Slots after cut-clip+rebase, before tighten-pace.

title: trim-filler + cut-filler skills
description: Two atomic skills for the per-clip editing chain. trim-filler: Claude reads a clip transcript and marks filler/trail-offs/false-starts; emits keeps.json + transcript.trimmed.json. cut-filler: re-encodes the clip via ffmpeg select/aselect using keeps.json. Slots after cut-clip+rebase, before tighten-pace.

title: trim-filler + cut-filler: semantic dead-air/filler removal
description: Two new atomic skills for the per-clip editing chain. trim-filler asks Claude to read a clip's word-timed transcript and mark filler words, trail-offs, false starts, and digressive asides — emitting keeps.json + transcript.trimmed.json. cut-filler consumes keeps.json and re-encodes the clip via ffmpeg select/aselect. Slots in after cut-clip+rebase, before tighten-pace.

title: trim-filler + cut-filler: semantic dead-air/filler removal
description: Two new atomic skills for the per-clip editing chain. trim-filler asks Claude to read a clip's word-timed transcript and mark filler words, trail-offs, false starts, and digressive asides — emitting keeps.json (keep ranges) + transcript.trimmed.json (shifted words). cut-filler consumes keeps.json and re-encodes the clip with ffmpeg select/aselect. Slots in after cut-clip+rebase, before tighten-pace.

title: bg-music skill: mix random ./songs/<cat>/ track under finished clip
description: New atomic skill at .claude/skills/bg-music. Picks random mp3/wav from ./songs/<category>/ (default ALL SONGS), loops to clip length, amix at vol=0.12 (~-18dB) under speech, alimiter at 0.97. Runs after loudnorm. Smoke-tested with Energetic/brodyaga funk.MP3 on clip_01.final.mp4. Not yet wired into shorts.sh.

title: wire tighten-pace + bg-music into shorts.sh
description: Insert tighten-pace after cut-clip+rebase (replacing transcript var used downstream). Insert bg-music between loudnorm and qc-clip. Verify a full pipeline pass on the mrbeast podcast still hits the canonical rules (1080x1920 + title card on first 2.5s).

title: tighten-pace skill: collapse inter-word dead air >0.25s
description: New atomic skill at .claude/skills/tighten-pace. Re-emits clip + retimed transcript. Smoke-tested on clip_01 (34.4s -> 30.2s, 4.18s removed). Not yet wired into shorts.sh — needs to run after cut-clip+rebase, before chunk-captions/fit-vertical, and downstream skills must use the new tightened transcript.

title: shorts.sh: wire new pipeline order with verify-coherence/chunk-captions/generate-title
description: Insert verify-coherence after pick-segments. Per-clip: chunk-captions before burn-subtitles; generate-title before title-transition.

title: title-transition: restyle to match caption brand (Impact + cyan/white)
description: Replace Arial Bold + yellow accent + dark panel with Impact + cyan/white + transparent (no panel) ALL-CAPS to exactly match burn-subtitles brand preset.

title: new skill: generate-title (per-clip, principles-based)
description: Reads clip transcript + ingest.json, infers subject, emits ALL-CAPS <=7-word third-person hook title. Split out of pick-segments (which stops emitting title_suggestion).

title: burn-subtitles: chunked rendering (kills scroll)
description: New 'chunks' style consumes chunks.json. One chunk on screen at a time, hard-cut between chunks, karaoke active-word highlight within chunk. Preserves Impact + cyan/white preset.

title: new skill: verify-coherence (post-pick gate)
description: After pick-segments, for each span Claude re-reads the span transcript and verdicts keep/tighten. Tighten returns trimmed [t0,t1] covering the dominant topic. Spans tightened below dmin are dropped silently.

title: new skill: chunk-captions (Claude-driven phrase chunks)
description: Reads clip-local transcript, Claude returns ordered chunks of {text,words:[{w,t0,t1}]}. Writes chunks.json. Replaces rolling-window caption rendering.

title: segment-topics: tighten boundaries prompt
description: Strengthen the segment-topics prompt to produce finer-grained topic boundaries so picked spans have less room to cross them. Same I/O contract.

title: shorts.sh: wire new canonical chain (hook-overlay + sfx-beats, drop title-transition)
description: Coordination bead. After the new skills land (hook-overlay, sfx-beats, redesigned burn-subtitles) and title-transition is removed, update shorts.sh canonical chain to: cut-clip → fit-vertical → burn-subtitles → hook-overlay → sfx-beats → loudnorm → qc-clip → save-local. Update CLAUDE.md 'Hard rules' section and the pipeline ascii diagram to match. If length-variants bead lands, the per-span loop also iterates over variants[].

title: new skill: sfx-beats (mid-clip risers/hits at tension peaks)
description: Per research, sound design is emotional manipulation, not decoration. Add a skill that places SFX events mid-clip at detected tension peaks. INPUTS: video, transcript.json (for but/therefore boundaries), audio_rms.json. OUTPUT: same video with SFX layered. RULES: (a) place a riser (0.6-1.0s synthesized noise sweep up) ending exactly at the first 'but' / 'therefore' / topic-pivot boundary detected in the transcript, (b) place a low impact hit (50-80Hz transient) at the single highest RMS peak after the riser, (c) place a soft outro stinger at the last 0.4s. Synthesize all SFX with stdlib wave module (same pattern as title-transition make_sfx.py) — no external assets. Mix at -18dB under speech. Skip placement if confidence is low (e.g. no detected boundary in middle 60% of clip). Replaces title-transition's whoosh as the engagement-sound mechanism.

title: new skill: hook-overlay (first-2s top-of-frame title-in-thumbnail)
description: Per Jenny Hoyos: 'the hook must work as a YouTube title in thumbnail — visually self-explanatory before audio plays.' Add a skill that overlays the segment's title_suggestion as a LARGE top-of-frame text card for the first 1.8-2.0 seconds of the short, then drops away. NOT a slide animation — appears with the first frame, holds, hard-cuts out. Uses the same redesigned caption font/color preset for brand consistency. Replaces title-transition in the canonical pipeline (see related bead on removing title-transition).
notes: Superseded by SPEC.md (commit df6ece3): title-transition kept and restyled to match captions; captions use Claude-chunked phrases (chunk-captions skill) instead of a rolling 4-word window.

title: pick-segments: emit length variants (30/34/45)
description: Per research, optimal short length is channel-specific. For each topic-coherent region, pick-segments should emit up to 3 candidate spans at different target durations (30s, 34s, 45s) anchored on the same hook, varying the tail. Downstream pipeline renders all variants so post-publish data can inform future length choice. Output schema: segments.shorts[i].variants[].{t0,t1,duration_target}.

title: Subtitle redesign: rolling 4-word window
description: Max 4 words on-screen at a time, only during speech, active word colored, pop in/out animation.
notes: Superseded by SPEC.md (commit df6ece3): title-transition kept and restyled to match captions; captions use Claude-chunked phrases (chunk-captions skill) instead of a rolling 4-word window.

title: Subtitle redesign: rolling 4-word window with pop in/out
description: Max 4 words visible at a time, only during speech, current word colored, pop-in animation as words enter and pop-out as they leave the window.
notes: Superseded by SPEC.md (commit df6ece3): title-transition kept and restyled to match captions; captions use Claude-chunked phrases (chunk-captions skill) instead of a rolling 4-word window.

title: Subtitle redesign: rolling 4-word window with pop in/out
description: User wants: max 4 words on screen at a time, only visible when speech is active, current word colored, words pop in as they're spoken and pop out as they leave the window.

title: Topic-coherent segment selection
description: Shorts cross topic boundaries within one clip because pick-segments has no notion of chapters. Add segment-topics skill (transcript -> topics.json) and constrain pick-segments to keep each span inside one topic. Wire into shorts.sh.

title: Topic-coherent segment selection
description: Shorts cross topic boundaries within one clip because pick-segments has no notion of chapters. Add segment-topics skill (transcript -> topics.json) and constrain pick-segments to keep each span inside one topic. Wire into shorts.sh.

title: Topic-coherent segment selection
description: Shorts cross topic boundaries within one clip because pick-segments has no notion of chapters. Add segment-topics skill (transcript -> topics.json) and constrain pick-segments to keep each span inside one topic. Wire into shorts.sh.

title: Topic-coherent segment selection
description: Shorts currently cross topic boundaries within a single clip because pick-segments has no notion of topical chapters. Add segment-topics skill (transcript -> topics.json with t0/t1/title/summary) and constrain pick-segments to keep each picked span inside exactly one topic. Wire into shorts.sh.

title: title-transition skill: animated intro title card
description: New atomic skill that overlays an animated title card on the first ~2.5s of a short. Brief title slides in from the left, holds centered, slides out the right, with a synthesized whoosh SFX. Renders title as a PIL PNG (no drawtext in local ffmpeg), moves it via ffmpeg overlay x-expression, synthesizes whoosh via pure-python WAV, mixes over source audio.
notes: Implemented .claude/skills/title-transition/ (SKILL.md, title-transition.sh, render_title.py, make_sfx.py). Tested on 6s 1080x1920 clip: slide-in/hold/slide-out verified frame-by-frame at t=0.15/0.45/1.0/2.25/2.45/3.0; whoosh-out -11.7dB vs -16dB hold; cache hit, no-audio branch, and short-title cases all pass.

title: fit-vertical skill: blurred-background 9:16 reframe (no face tracking)
description: Convert a 16:9 YouTube-aspect video to 9:16 shorts format without facial tracking. Whole video scaled to fit, centered, with a blurred zoomed copy filling top/bottom. Simpler counterpart to reframe-vertical which requires a speaker track.

title: Make shorts from IShowSpeed compilation pA6ls-oHhX0
description: Run the full shorts.sh pipeline on https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pA6ls-oHhX0 (16:20 IShowSpeed funny moments comp). Produce vertical 9:16 shorts under ./output/.

title: Make shorts from IShowSpeed compilation pA6ls-oHhX0
description: Run the full shorts.sh pipeline on https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pA6ls-oHhX0 (16:20 IShowSpeed funny moments comp). Produce vertical 9:16 shorts under ./output/.

title: Implement cut-clip skill (ffmpeg trim)
description: Thin ffmpeg wrapper. Stream-copy by default, re-encode flag for frame-accurate cuts. See .claude/skills/cut-clip/SKILL.md.
notes:
Implemented .claude/skills/cut-clip/cut-clip.sh. Tested on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (Big Buck Bunny):
- stream-copy [10,18]: ffprobe duration=9.642667 (keyframe-aligned, expected)
- reencode=true [10,18]: ffprobe duration=8.000000 (frame-accurate)
- re-run with same args: cache hit (mtime check)
Both outputs 3840x2160 video + audio streams intact.

title: Implement burn-subtitles skill (ASS + ffmpeg)
description: Generate ASS subtitle file from word timestamps (line / word-karaoke / selective modes), burn via ffmpeg subtitles= filter. Salvage ASS templating from archive/pre-pivot:pipeline_v2.py:339-512. See .claude/skills/burn-subtitles/SKILL.md.
notes:
burn-subtitles works. Local ffmpeg 8.1 (homebrew) has NO libass/subtitles/drawtext
filter, so subtitles are rendered as a transparent PNG-per-frame sequence (PIL,
Arial Bold, white+black-stroke, lower third) and composited via ffmpeg overlay.
Re-encodes video libx264 veryfast crf18; audio stream-copied.

Tested on 6.7s 720x1280 say-synthesized clip (19 words, transcript via transcribe skill):
  word-karaoke: 20 unique states; frame@3.2s lower-third = 16130 white + 5616 accent px
  line:         4 unique states;  frame@3.2s lower-third = 27049 white + 0 accent px
Output dims 720x1280 preserved, duration 6.726s preserved, audio aac copied.
Cache hit verified on re-run. Styles: line | word-karaoke | selective.

title: Implement reframe-vertical skill (ffmpeg crop path)
description: Apply speaker-track to source video as smooth 9:16 crop. Compute crop centers, smooth with one-euro filter, render via ffmpeg sendcmd or filter expressions. Salvage smoothing logic from archive/pre-pivot:pipeline_v2.py:178-238. See .claude/skills/reframe-vertical/SKILL.md.
close_reason: Implemented and tested against work/ artifacts

title: Implement detect-faces skill (MediaPipe)
description: Sample frames at configurable fps, run MediaPipe Face Detector, emit per-timestamp boxes JSON. See .claude/skills/detect-faces/SKILL.md.
notes: Tested on a 15s 320x240 talking-head clip (yt-dlp slice of UF8uR6Z6KLc, 30-45s): 75 frames sampled at 5fps, 73 with detected face boxes. First box: x=125 y=40 w=47 h=47 score=0.77. Runtime ~1s. Output schema matches SKILL.md. Idempotent via mtime cache.

title: Implement pick-speaker skill (Claude vision)
description: For each transcript span, identify the active-speaker face box. Single face -> trivial. Multiple faces -> sample 2-3 keyframes + transcript text, ask Claude (host session or /crew member) to pick numbered face. Output speaker_box per span. See .claude/skills/pick-speaker/SKILL.md.
close_reason: Implemented and tested against work/ artifacts

title: Add hello.md with the word "hi"
description:
Dispatched from GitHub issue jperrello/shorts#1. Body:\n\nCreate a new file at the repo root named `hello.md`. Its contents should be the single word: hi

That's all — no other content, no other files. A trailing newline is fine.

title: Implement save-local skill (per-source folder)
description: Read OUTPUT_DIR from .env, mkdir -p <OUTPUT_DIR>/<source-stem>, copy in. Return destination path. See .claude/skills/save-local/SKILL.md.
close_reason: Implemented and tested against work/ artifacts

title: Implement qc-clip skill (ffprobe sanity)
description: ffprobe duration + size. Return verdict JSON with pass bool and reason. See .claude/skills/qc-clip/SKILL.md.
close_reason: Implemented and tested against work/ artifacts

title: Implement loudnorm skill (ffmpeg two-pass)
description: Measure pass + apply pass with measured params. Stream-copy video. Targets I=-14 TP=-1.5 LRA=11 by default. See .claude/skills/loudnorm/SKILL.md.
notes:
Implemented .claude/skills/loudnorm/loudnorm.sh. Two-pass: measure → parse JSON from ffmpeg stderr → apply with measured params (linear=true). Video -c copy, audio -c:a aac -b:a 192k. Idempotent via mtime.

Test: cut-clip on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 [30,40] → /tmp/loudnorm_test_in.mp4 → loudnorm → /tmp/loudnorm_test_out.mp4.
Measured input: I=-31.45 TP=-17.46 LRA=8.90 thresh=-41.51 offset=-2.15.
Verification ffmpeg loudnorm on output: input_i=-11.78, input_tp=0.22 (peak limited apply gives output_tp=-1.50 exact). Drift ~2 LU from -14 target on this quiet short clip — within expected two-pass tolerance.

value: cut-clip skill works: .claude/skills/cut-clip/cut-clip.sh <input> <t0> <t1> <out> [reencode]. Stream-copy default (keyframe-aligned, ~1s slop), reencode=true gives frame-accurate via libx264 veryfast crf18. Idempotent via mtime cache. Tested on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 [10,18]: stream-copy=9.64s, reencode=8.000s exact.

pick-segments-hook-scoring: shorts-0kq complete. build_prompt.py now asks Claude to score hook_score/structure_score/overall_score (0-10) per span, requires first-1-2s scroll-stop strength, hook→foreshadow→payoff, but/therefore causality. parse_reply.py rejects spans whose first transcript word(s) match filler list (so/and/um/like/I think/basically/...) and sorts picks by overall_score before topping to N. Evidence: ran .claude/skills/pick-segments/pick-segments.sh work/883ad50ade/transcript.json /tmp/segments_test.json 3 20 60 → output JSON has hook_score/structure_score/overall_score fields on every span (grep confirmed 9 score lines for 3 spans). Single Claude call preserved.

broll-mcptube-wiring: mcptube discover REQUIRES an LLM API key ('Discovery requires an LLM. Set an API key.') — unusable in the no-API-key stack. Workaround: keyless discovery via bundled yt-dlp 'ytsearchN:<query>' --flat-playlist --print '%(id)s|%(title)s|%(duration)s'. mcptube add/frame/remove all work keyless. add url ingests (video_id==YouTube id, recorded for cleanup). frame <id> <ts> writes ~/.mcptube/frames/<id>_<ts>.jpg and prints the path. remove <id> evicts. Round-trip verified on zTOljGXyL-c (35s hippo clip).

ingest skill works: yt-dlp -> work/<id>/source.mp4 + ingest.json. Tested on Big Buck Bunny (aqz-KE-bpKQ) → 709MB mp4, duration 634.624s, 3840x2160 @ 60fps. id is sha1(url)[:10]. Idempotent: cache-hits when source.mp4 + ingest.json exist for same URL. Script: .claude/skills/ingest/ingest.sh

transcribe skill works: .claude/skills/transcribe/transcribe.sh <input> [out] [lang]. Uses whisper-cli --max-len 1 --split-on-word --output-json-full for word timestamps; ffmpeg extracts 16kHz mono wav first. Sources .env (WHISPER_BIN, WHISPER_MODEL must be quoted since model path has a space). Tested on synthesized 'say' speech (8.5s) → 23 words + 3 sentence segments. Idempotent via mtime check.
Target output: vertical 9:16 shorts (1080x1920), burned subtitles, loudnorm to broadcast spec, saved under ./out/<source-id>/clip-N.mp4.
Each skill must be independently invocable. Re-running a skill should be idempotent if inputs unchanged. No skill bundles two operations.

detect-faces skill works: .claude/skills/detect-faces/detect-faces.sh <input> [out] [fps=5]. Wraps detect_faces.py (MediaPipe FaceDetector VIDEO mode with blaze_face_short_range.tflite). Outputs JSON: {source,fps,width,height,duration,frames:[{t,boxes:[{x,y,w,h,score}]}]}. Idempotent via mtime cache. Tested on 15s 320x240 talking-head slice (yt-dlp UF8uR6Z6KLc 30-45s): 75 frames @ 5fps, 73 with faces, ~1s runtime. Note: BBB is animation, no face hits — needs real human faces.

fill-vertical skill (shorts-zeq): punch-in 9:16 reframe replacing fit-vertical+reframe-vertical (both deleted). .claude/skills/fill-vertical/{SKILL.md,fill_vertical.py,fill-vertical.sh,models/face_landmarker.task}. Invoke: fill-vertical.sh <input> <out> [target=1080x1920] [face_frac=0.45] [max_zoom=2.0] [scene_thresh=0.4] [samples=5]. Pipeline: ffprobe -> ffmpeg scene-detect (select gt(scene,thresh) on 480p decode) -> per-shot sample K frames -> MediaPipe FaceLandmarker (model bundled, indices 13/14 inner lips, 10/152 face extent) -> link faces by center proximity -> speaker=max mouth-openness variance track (tie->biggest+central); no face -> cv2.saliency.StaticSaliencyFineGrained centroid cover-crop. Face framed face_frac of height, eyeline upper third, upscale capped at max_zoom. Render per-shot crop->scale->concat-demuxer, audio stream-copied whole clip, +faststart, encode via _lib/encode.sh (low). Idempotent mtime cache. Deps already in system python3.14: mediapipe 0.10.33, opencv-contrib-python 4.13, numpy 2.4. model force-added (.gitignore ignores models/). TESTED clip_01 (6 shots, faces): 16s for 32s clip, out 1080x1920 aac dur 32.282250 exact, contact sheet=full-bleed faces ~45% no blur bars. clip_03 (face+2 saliency shots): out 1080x1920 dur 35.994292 exact. cache-hit verified.

sfx-beats skill works: .claude/skills/sfx-beats/sfx-beats.sh <video> <transcript.json> <out> [rms.json]. Pipeline: plan_sfx.py scans transcript for pivot words (but/therefore/so/because/however/then/actually) in middle 60% of clip; if found -> riser ends at pivot t, hit lands at post-pivot RMS-per-second peak; stinger always in last 0.4s. SFX synthesized via stdlib wave at ~-18 dBFS (PEAK=0.126). Verified on work/883ad50ade/clip_03.final.mp4 (57.9s, no pivot -> ok=False, stinger-only path) AND a synthetic-pivot variant where plan emitted {riser_start:24.2, riser_end:25.0, hit_t:25.6, stinger_t:57.5}. SFX bed probe (wave.open + RMS in 200ms windows): FULL bed -> peak=0.119 at riser end, peak=0.139 at hit_t, peak=0.091 at stinger; NOPIV bed -> 0 at t=10/25, peak=0.091 only at stinger. Output mp4: h264 stream-copied + aac 2ch re-encoded, 18.35MB, duration preserved at 57.900000s. ffmpeg amix with alimiter=0.97. Idempotent via <out>.sfxmeta key (input+transcript mtimes). Mid-clip RMS deltas in the rendered mp4 are dwarfed by AAC re-encoding noise (~0.003 baseline) — verify by probing the SFX wav directly, not the final mp4 1s buckets.

title-transition skill works: .claude/skills/title-transition/title-transition.sh <input> <title> <out> [dur=2.5] [font_size=auto]. Overlays an animated title card on the clip's opening seconds (duration unchanged). render_title.py: PIL PNG banner — Arial Bold, auto-sized to largest fit in <=2 lines, UPPERCASE, dark rounded panel + accent-yellow (255,214,51) border. Slid by ffmpeg overlay x-expression: ease-out-cubic slide-in from left, hold centered mid-frame, ease-in-cubic slide-out right; enable='between(t,0,dur)'. make_sfx.py: pure-python SFX (stdlib wave) — one-pole-lowpass filtered noise, rising whoosh + low sine impact + falling whoosh, panned L->R, mixed via amix normalize=0 + alimiter. CRITICAL: NO -loop 1 for the PNG (infinite image input hangs ffmpeg) — a single still PNG is held by overlay's repeatlast. Idempotent via <out>.ttmeta signature (title|dur|font). Tested on 6s 1080x1920 gameplay clip: animation verified frame-by-frame, whoosh-out -11.7dB vs -16dB hold, no-audio branch makes SFX the track.
Claude-driven skills (pick-segments, pick-speaker) call Claude through the host session or a /crew member, never the API. Inputs: JSON paths. Outputs: JSON to disk.

/start pane driver: tick-poll, not sentinel. run_claude_step in .claude/skills/_lib/pane.sh polls out.txt every PANE_TICK seconds (default 6) and considers a round settled when size is non-empty AND unchanged across one tick. Replaces out.done touch which raced under parallel orchestrator load. shorts-tnd fix.

verify-bookends + trim-filler tightening + tighten-pace defaults: shipped. New verify-bookends skill at .claude/skills/verify-bookends/ runs after tighten-pace, before fit-vertical; ffprobe + 3-frame hstack each end + claude -p with embedded image paths; returns keep|trim|drop. Validation downgrades trims that would remove >2s to drop and trims that would shrink <15s to keep. trim-filler/build_prompt.py now declares standard filler always-removable and demands aggressive aside removal; parse_reply.py falls back to no-op if kept sum > input duration and warns above 40% removed. tighten-pace defaults: gap_max=0.18, sentence_beat=0.15 (when prev word ends . ? !), collapse_to=0.08; env TIGHTEN_GAP/TIGHTEN_SENTENCE_BEAT/TIGHTEN_COLLAPSE_TO. Plan.py half-pads each side of a long gap so residual silence equals target. shorts.sh wired (already committed in d581824).

burn-subtitles skill works: .claude/skills/burn-subtitles/burn-subtitles.sh <input> <transcript.json> <out> [style=word-karaoke|line|selective] [font_size=72]. IMPORTANT: local ffmpeg 8.1 has NO libass/subtitles/drawtext filter — subtitles are rendered as a transparent PNG-per-frame sequence via PIL (burn_subtitles.py, Arial Bold white+black-stroke, lower third, 9:16-safe margins) then composited with ffmpeg overlay. Video re-encoded libx264 veryfast crf18, audio stream-copied. word-karaoke highlights active word in accent yellow. selective burns only segments overlapping RMS z>1.0 seconds. Idempotent via mtime. Tested 6.7s 720x1280 clip: karaoke frame@3.2s = 16130 white + 5616 accent px in lower third.

like-subscribe-overlay is now JS-rendered. Source: .claude/skills/like-subscribe-overlay/cta.html (HTML+CSS+JS with deterministic window.setT(t) driver for cursor, click punches, bell ring, thumb fill). Build pipeline: build-cta.sh -> npm i playwright (one-time) -> playwright install chromium -> node build-cta.js (steps setT and screenshots each frame with omitBackground=true) -> ffmpeg -c:v prores_ks -pix_fmt yuva444p10le -profile:v 4444 -> assets/cta.mov (3.0s, 1080x320, transparent alpha). Runtime: like-subscribe-overlay.sh composites cta.mov via setpts=PTS*sf+start/TB (stretches to dur and offsets to clip_dur-dur), scale=92% clip-w, overlay at lower-third y=(H-h)-H*0.14. Bell SFX layered. The old PIL banner (render_cta.py) and the direct gif overlay are obsolete; gif kept at project root as visual reference. To re-author: edit cta.html, run bash build-cta.sh, commit assets/cta.mov.

pick-segments skill works: .claude/skills/pick-segments/pick-segments.sh <transcript.json> [out] [n=5] [dmin=20] [dmax=60]. Pipeline: rms.py (ffmpeg s16le 8kHz mono -> per-second RMS) -> build_prompt.py (timestamped transcript + 60-bin sparkline) -> claude -p --output-format text -> parse_reply.py (regex JSON, validate non-overlap+duration). Writes {source, shorts:[{t0,t1,rationale,title_suggestion}]}. Idempotent via mtime. Uses host claude subscription (NO API key). Tested on synthetic 110s/10-segment transcript: 2 valid spans (0-38.5, 47-95). RMS verified on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (635 buckets).

like-subscribe-overlay skill replaced its synthesized PIL banner with the actual YouTube subscribe-button click animation. Asset: .claude/skills/like-subscribe-overlay/assets/cta.gif (1600x1200, 7.16s, copied from project-root gif.gif). Pipeline: ffmpeg -ignore_loop 1 -i gif, then setpts=PTS*(dur/gif_dur)+start/TB time-stretches to fit dur AND offsets to start; scale=82%-of-clip-width; colorkey=0xFFFFFF:0.18:0.06 punches out white background; format=yuva420p preserves alpha through overlay. Positioned (W-w)/2, (H-h)-(H*0.18) — lower third. render_cta.py is now unused (kept on disk for reference). title-transition default duration tightened from 2.5s to 1.4s for faster pace.

fit-vertical skill works: .claude/skills/fit-vertical/fit-vertical.sh <input> <out> [target=1080x1920] [blur_sigma=20]. Aspect-ratio-only 9:16 reframe, NO face tracking (counterpart to reframe-vertical which needs a speaker track). Single ffmpeg -filter_complex pass: split source -> bg scaled increase+crop+gblur sigma -> fg scaled decrease to fit -> overlay centered, setsar=1. Video reencoded libx264 veryfast crf18 yuv420p, audio stream-copied. Idempotent via out-vs-input mtime. Tested on 8s 3840x2160 BBB slice -> 1080x1920, 8.0s preserved, ~8s runtime.

One atomic operation per skill. Skills live at .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with frontmatter contract. Data between skills passes as JSON files on disk under ./work/<id>/ (transcript.json, faces.json, speaker.json, segments.json).

/start orchestrator now drives semantic steps through long-lived Claude tmux panes with SHORTS_PANE_MODE=chat. start.sh launches bash panes for srcprep/mcptube and Claude panes for analysis/editor/captions/completion lanes. _lib/pane.sh sends file-based tasks to interactive Claude panes: read in.txt, write out.txt, touch out.done; fallback remains claude -p when no pane or non-chat mode. Clear at lane boundaries, preserve context within one source/span. verify-bookends and pick-mood now source pane.sh and accept --pane.

title-transition skill: title POPS IN at center via scale animation (NOT slide-in from side). Scale S(t): 0.3 -> ~1.07 (back-out Penner overshoot) -> 1.0 during fly window, holds at 1, then scales to 0 with p^1.5 ease-in. ffmpeg input uses '-loop 1 -t dur' on the PNG so scale=eval=frame re-evaluates per frame. Impact effects fire at landing frame (t=fly) on underlying clip BEFORE title overlay: white flash (eq=brightness 0.38 -> 0 over 70ms), screen shake (pad+crop damped sinusoid +-9px X / +-5px Y, 150ms). Layered impact SFX: 30ms filtered-noise thwack + 160ms 95Hz tom + 450ms 80Hz->38Hz sub-bass drop. Pipeline wiring unchanged - title-transition still runs after burn-subtitles, before loudnorm; like-subscribe-overlay runs after broll.

broll-split: shipped .claude/skills/broll-pick + broll-composite. broll-pick: Claude anchor-pick + chunk-snap + Pexels top-3 + 3-frame grid vision-verify (claude -p) + query rewrite fallback + BROLL_VISION_CAP=10 default. Emits broll_plan.json. broll-composite: pure ffmpeg overlay y=1332 1080x520. shorts.sh wired. Verified work/81d2d55a40/clip_01: 7 picks, 8 vision calls, 1 rewrite, 39.9s 1080x1920 brolled output.

burn-subtitles-brand-preset: font=Impact (/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Impact.ttf), active-word color=electric cyan #00E5FF (rgba 0,229,255), context color=off-white #F5F5F0, position=upper-third (top of first line at y=0.22*H), animation=8px slide-up over 80ms linear + hard-cut exit (no ease-out-back, no scale). Stroke=fs/10 black. Verified on work/subtest/out_karaoke.mp4 frame@3.2s: cyan_px=8049 at y=[297,354] of 1280 (=23% from top), white text 9186px all in upper half, MrBeast-yellow px=0. SKILL.md documents this as deliberate brand identity.

loudnorm skill works: .claude/skills/loudnorm/loudnorm.sh <input> <out> [I=-14] [TP=-1.5] [LRA=11]. Two-pass ffmpeg loudnorm — measure pass parses JSON from stderr, apply pass uses measured_I/TP/LRA/thresh/offset with linear=true. Video stream-copied, audio reencoded to AAC 192k. Idempotent via mtime cache. Tested on 10s slice of work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (BBB intro, quiet -31.45 LUFS): output measured -11.78 LUFS / TP -1.50 dBTP. Short/quiet clips drift ~2 LU from target — expected.

pick-mood + bg-music: pick-mood skill (.claude/skills/pick-mood/) reads a clip transcript and asks Claude to choose one ./songs/<mood>/ folder from the live filesystem list, falling back to ALL SONGS on failure. bg-music.sh now (1) recurses across all mood folders when category=ALL SONGS instead of erroring on a missing folder, and (2) keeps ./songs/.recent (last 5 picked tracks) and excludes them from the next random pick. shorts.sh wires pick-mood between like-subscribe-overlay and bg-music, passing the chosen mood as bg-music's category arg. Songs are instrumental, so mood routing judges vibe only — no lyric concerns.
Stack: whisper.cpp (local transcribe) + Claude (via host session or /crew tmux, NO API key) + ffmpeg + MediaPipe. Binary paths in .env. Never use Anthropic API directly.
